technical_specs.parameter: Inducer start on HIGH speed and low pressure switch closes duration (W1 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.1 Low Fire Heating Mode Logic (only W1 signal):
source_refs.source_quote: When there is a call for heat (W1), the inducer will start on HIGH speed and the low pressure switch closes. This will last for 15s.

technical_specs.parameter: Inducer to low speed and ignitor energize preheating sequence duration (W1 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.1 Low Fire Heating Mode Logic (only W1 signal):
source_refs.source_quote: Inducer will turn to low speed and ignitor will energize. This preheating sequence lasts for 17s.

technical_specs.parameter: Ignitor turn off after gas valve opens and burners light duration (W1 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.1 Low Fire Heating Mode Logic (only W1 signal):
source_refs.source_quote: After preheating, gas valve opens and the burners light. The ignitor will turn off after 3s.

technical_specs.parameter: Time delay before blower motor starts (W1 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.1 Low Fire Heating Mode Logic (only W1 signal):
source_refs.source_quote: There is 30s time delay before blower motor starts.

technical_specs.parameter: Post-purge duration (W1 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.1 Low Fire Heating Mode Logic (only W1 signal):
source_refs.source_quote: When there is no call for heat (no W1 call) and no flame is sensed, post-purge begins. This will last for 15s.

technical_specs.parameter: Fan delay to dissipate heat (W1 signal, default)

source_refs.section_title: A1.1 Low Fire Heating Mode Logic (only W1 signal):
source_refs.source_quote: There is a fan delay to dissipate heat in the system. This time depends on the dip switch S2-1 & S2-2. The default time is 90s.

technical_specs.parameter: Inducer start on HIGH speed and low pressure switch closes duration (W1+W2 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: When there is a call for heat (W1), the inducer will start on HIGH speed and the low pressure switch closes. This will last for 15s.

technical_specs.parameter: Inducer to low speed and ignitor energize preheating sequence duration (W1+W2 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: Inducer will turn to low speed and ignitor will energize. This preheating sequence lasts for 17s.

technical_specs.parameter: Ignitor turn off after gas valve opens and burners light duration (W1+W2 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: After preheating, gas valve opens and the burners light. The ignitor will turn off after 3s.

technical_specs.parameter: Delay before gas valve, inducer, pressure switch turn to high stage (W1+W2 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: Before gas valve, inducer, pressure switch turn to high stage, there is a 5s delay.

technical_specs.parameter: Time delay before blower motor starts (W1+W2 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: There is a time delay of 30s before blower motor starts.

technical_specs.parameter: Time delay before inducer shuts down (W1+W2 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: When there is no call for heat, there is a time delay before inducer shuts down. This will last for 15s.

technical_specs.parameter: Blower motor run at high speed for fan delay (W1+W2 signal)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: The blower motor will run at high speed for 30s for fan delay.

technical_specs.parameter: Fan delay to dissipate heat (W1+W2 signal, default)

source_refs.section_title: A1.2 High Fire Heating Mode Logic (W1+W2 Signal):
source_refs.source_quote: There is a fan delay to dissipate heat in the system. This time depends on the dip switch S2-1 & S2-2. The default time is 90s.

technical_specs.parameter: Inducer start on HIGH speed and low pressure switch closes duration (Ignition Failure)

source_refs.section_title: A2 Ignition Failure and Reignition Sequence
source_refs.source_quote: When there is a call for heat (W1), the inducer will start on HIGH speed and the low pressure switch closes. This will last for 15s.

technical_specs.parameter: Inducer to low speed and ignitor energize preheating sequence duration (Ignition Failure)

source_refs.section_title: A2 Ignition Failure and Reignition Sequence
source_refs.source_quote: Inducer will turn to low speed and ignitor will energize. This preheating sequence lasts for 17s.

technical_specs.parameter: Ignitor turn off after gas valve opens and burners light duration (Ignition Failure)

source_refs.section_title: A2 Ignition Failure and Reignition Sequence
source_refs.source_quote: After preheating, gas valve opens and the burners light. The ignitor will turn off after 3s.

technical_specs.parameter: Gas valve open duration if no flame (Ignition Failure)

source_refs.section_title: A2 Ignition Failure and Reignition Sequence
source_refs.source_quote: The gas valve will open for 4s if there is no flame.

technical_specs.parameter: Inducer run at high speed duration (Ignition Failure)

source_refs.section_title: A2 Ignition Failure and Reignition Sequence
source_refs.source_quote: The inducer will run at high speed for 60s.

technical_specs.parameter: Preheating duration (Ignition Failure)

source_refs.section_title: A2 Ignition Failure and Reignition Sequence
source_refs.source_quote: Preheating occurs for 27s.

technical_specs.parameter: Ignitor turn off after preheating, gas valve opens duration (Ignition Failure)

source_refs.section_title: A2 Ignition Failure and Reignition Sequence
source_refs.source_quote: After preheating, gas valve opens. The ignitor will turn off after 3s.

fault_codes.code: No Displayed Codes and No Fan
possible_causes: Blower access door not closed or fixed properly
No power to the circulating fan motor
Thermostat settings incorrect or faulty thermostat
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to wiring diagram
Check for 115Vac power supply between L1 and common terminal (Figure 1)
Check for 18-31Vac power between these 2 terminals with multi-meter (Figure 3)
Replace the door switch
Ensure the blower access door is closed properly
Replace the transformer
Replace the fuse
Check for 18-31Vac power between "R" & "C" terminals with multi-meter (Figure 4)
Check the incoming power supply at connection box
Replace the control board
Check for 18-31Vac power between "W/W1" & "C" terminals with multi-meter (Figure 5)
Check for 18-31Vac power between "G" & "C" terminals with multi-meter (Figure 6)
Check the thermostat settings and/or replace the thermostat
Check for 18-31Vac power between Hi-heat/lo-heat/hi-cool/lo-cool and COM with multi-meter (Figure 7)
Check for 115Vac power supply between CIRC-H and N with multi-meter (Figure 7)
Replace the circulating fan motor

source_refs.section_title: 4.1 No Displayed Codes and No Fan (System Does Not Start Normally)
source_refs.source_quote: No Displayed Codes and No Fan (System Does Not Start Normally)

source_refs.section_title: No Displayed Codes and No Fan (System Does Not Start Normally) Figures & Tables
source_refs.source_quote: 1. Check for 115Vac power supply between L1 and common terminal
1. Check for 18-31Vac power between "W/W1" & "C" terminals with multi-meter

fault_codes.description: System Lock-Out due to Failed Ignition
possible_causes: Flame not reaching the flame sensor
Manifold gas pressure not meeting requirement
Inlet gas pressure not meeting requirement
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to wiring diagram
Turn the gas valve switch to 'On'
Replace the ignitor
Check the wire connections between ignitor and PCB Board
Clean with steel wool or replace the flame sensor
Adjust gas valve to ensure it meets gas pressure requirement
Replace the gas valve if the pressure cannot be adjusted
Adjust the inlet gas pressure
Remove the blockage
Replace the PCB board
cautions_or_notes: The flame sense signal should be 0.5 - 6 microamps.

source_refs.section_title: 4.2 E7 (System Lock-Out due to Failed Ignition) E8 (System Lock-Out due to too Many Flame Dropouts)
source_refs.source_quote: E7 (System Lock-Out due to Failed Ignition)

fault_codes.description: System Lock-Out due to too Many Flame Dropouts
possible_causes: Flame not reaching the flame sensor
Manifold gas pressure not meeting requirement
Inlet gas pressure not meeting requirement
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to wiring diagram
Turn the gas valve switch to 'On'
Replace the ignitor
Check the wire connections between ignitor and PCB Board
Clean with steel wool or replace the flame sensor
Adjust gas valve to ensure it meets gas pressure requirement
Replace the gas valve if the pressure cannot be adjusted
Adjust the inlet gas pressure
Remove the blockage
Replace the PCB board
cautions_or_notes: The flame sense signal should be 0.5 - 6 microamps.

source_refs.section_title: 4.2 E7 (System Lock-Out due to Failed Ignition) E8 (System Lock-Out due to too Many Flame Dropouts)
source_refs.source_quote: E8 (System Lock-Out due to too Many Flame Dropouts)

fault_codes.description: Low Fire Pressure Switch Stuck Closed
possible_causes: Low fire pressure switch stuck closed
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to the wiring diagram
Replace the pressure switch
Replace the PCB Board
Check vent for obstructions
cautions_or_notes: This error could also be caused by a blocked vent. Check vent for obstructions. If a vent is blocked, unit's safety protection logic will turn off unit.

source_refs.section_title: 4.3 E1 (Low Fire Pressure Switch Stuck Closed)
source_refs.source_quote: E1 (Low Fire Pressure Switch Stuck Closed)

possible_causes: Blockage in the pressure hose or loose hose
Incorrect pressure at the pressure switch
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to the wiring diagram
Replace the PCB Board
Remove blockage or fix the pressure hose
Remove blockage in the exhaust pipe
Replace the inducer
Replace the pressure switch
cautions_or_notes: The pressure switch that you are checking in this step depends on the fault code displayed on the LCD screen. Refer to the code descriptions above for troubleshooting steps.

source_refs.section_title: 4.4 E2 (Low Pressure Switch Stuck Open) E4 (Pressure Switch Cycle Lockout) E3 (High Pressure Switch Stuck Open)

Remove blockage in the exhaust pipe
Replace the inducer
Replace the pressure switch
cautions_or_notes: The pressure switch that you are checking in this step depends on the fault code displayed on the LCD screen. Refer to the code descriptions above for troubleshooting steps.

source_refs.section_title: 4.4 E2 (Low Pressure Switch Stuck Open) E4 (Pressure Switch Cycle Lockout) E3 (High Pressure Switch Stuck Open)

fault_codes.description: Limit/Rollout Switch Open Less than 5 Mins
possible_causes: Rollout limit switch showing resistance other than '0'
Chamber/fan mounted limit switches not in right modes
Circulating fan motor not turning on after 30s delay
Circulating fan motor speed not correctly set
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to wiring diagram
Turn off the equipment for 10 minutes to ensure sufficient cooling off period. Measure the resistance between two leads on the limit switches
Replace the (chamber/fan mounted) Limit Switch
Replace the PCB Board
Remove blockage in the exhaust pipe
Replace the Rollout Switch
Manual reset of the limit switch (by pressing the button on switch)
Replace the Limit Switch
Replace the circulating fan motor
Clean/Replace the filter
Check the airflow duct for blockage
Correct the circulating fan motor speed or replace the motor if necessary
cautions_or_notes: "SW2" is used for the fan delay after burner starts.
Verify that the temperature rise is between 40-55°F.

source_refs.section_title: 4.5 E5 (Limit/Rollout Switch Open Less than 5 Mins) E6 (Limit/Rollout Switch Open More than 5 Mins)
source_refs.source_quote: E5 (Limit/Rollout Switch Open Less than 5 Mins)

fault_codes.description: Limit/Rollout Switch Open More than 5 Mins
possible_causes: Rollout limit switch showing resistance other than '0'
Chamber/fan mounted limit switches not in right modes
Circulating fan motor not turning on after 30s delay
Circulating fan motor speed not correctly set
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to wiring diagram
Turn off the equipment for 10 minutes to ensure sufficient cooling off period. Measure the resistance between two leads on the limit switches
Replace the (chamber/fan mounted) Limit Switch
Replace the PCB Board
Remove blockage in the exhaust pipe
Replace the Rollout Switch
Manual reset of the limit switch (by pressing the button on switch)
Replace the Limit Switch
Replace the circulating fan motor
Clean/Replace the filter
Check the airflow duct for blockage
Correct the circulating fan motor speed or replace the motor if necessary
cautions_or_notes: "SW2" is used for the fan delay after burner starts.
Verify that the temperature rise is between 40-55°F.

source_refs.section_title: 4.5 E5 (Limit/Rollout Switch Open Less than 5 Mins) E6 (Limit/Rollout Switch Open More than 5 Mins)
source_refs.source_quote: E6 (Limit/Rollout Switch Open More than 5 Mins)

fault_codes.description: Flame Sensed with Gas Valve Off
possible_causes: Loose wires or wrong connections for flame sensor
Flame sensor not sitting in correct location
manufacturer_steps: Correct wiring according to the wiring diagram
Correct the flame sensor location
Restart system
Replace flame sensor
Replace control board

source_refs.section_title: 4.6 FE (Flame Sensed with Gas Valve Off)
source_refs.source_quote: FE (Flame Sensed with Gas Valve Off)

possible_causes: Incorrect wire connection (black and white wire)
manufacturer_steps: Correct the wire connection (black and white wire) according to wiring diagram. See Figure 24.

source_refs.section_title: 4.7 PR (Incorrect Polarity of L1/L2)
source_refs.source_quote: PR (Incorrect Polarity of L1/L2)

possible_causes: Manifold gas pressure not meeting requirement
Inlet gas pressure not meeting requirement
manufacturer_steps: Adjust the gas valve to ensure manifold gas pressure meets the required values
Replace the gas valve if the pressure cannot be adjusted
Adjust the inlet gas pressure
Clean with steel wool or replace the Flame Sensor
Properly ground the PCB Board
Remove the blockage
Replace the PCB Board

safety_warnings.text: Untrained personnel (homeowners) may only clean and replace filters and replace fuses as required by basic maintenance. All other operations, including installation, repair, and service must be performed by a qualified installer, service agency, or the gas supplier.

source_refs.section_title: 1.2 Safety
source_refs.source_quote: Untrained personnel (homeowners) may only clean and replace filters and replace fuses as required by basic maintenance. All other operations, including installation, repair, and service must be performed by a qualified installer, service agency, or the gas supplier.

safety_warnings.text: Do not store or use gasoline or other flammable vapors and liquids in the vicinity of this or any other appliance.

source_refs.section_title: 1.2 Safety
source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FIRE OR EXPLOSION HAZARD Do not store or use gasoline or other flammable vapors and liquids in the vicinity of this or any other appliance.

safety_warnings.text: WHAT TO DO IF YOU SMELL GAS: Do not try to light any appliance. Do not touch any electrical switch; do not use any phone in your building. Leave the building immediately. Immediately call your gas supplier from a neighbor's phone. Follow the gas supplier's instructions. If you cannot reach your gas supplier, call the fire department. Installation and service must be performed by a qualified installer, service agency, or the gas supplier.

source_refs.section_title: 1.2 Safety
source_refs.source_quote: WHAT TO DO IF YOU SMELL GAS: Do not try to light any appliance. Do not touch any electrical switch; do not use any phone in your building. Leave the building immediately. Immediately call your gas supplier from a neighbor's phone. Follow the gas supplier's instructions. If you cannot reach your gas supplier, call the fire department. Installation and service must be performed by a qualified installer, service agency, or the gas supplier.

safety_warnings.text: Do not use this furnace if any part has been under water. A flood-damaged furnace is extremely dangerous. Attempts to use the furnace can result in fire or explosion. A qualified service agent must inspect the furnace and replace all gas controls, control system parts, and electrical parts that have been wet, or the furnace if deemed necessary.

source_refs.section_title: 1.2 Safety
source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: Do not use this furnace if any part has been under water. A flood-damaged furnace is extremely dangerous. Attempts to use the furnace can result in fire or explosion. A qualified service agent must inspect the furnace and replace all gas controls, control system parts, and electrical parts that have been wet, or the furnace if deemed necessary.

safety_warnings.topic: Fire or Explosion Hazard - Fuel Type
safety_warnings.text: The furnace is designed and approved for use with Natural Gas and Propane (LP) Gas ONLY. DO NOT BURN ANY LIQUID FUEL OR SOLID FUEL IN THIS FURNACE. Burning any unapproved fuel will result in damage to the furnace's heat exchanger, which could result in Fire, Personal Injury, and/or Property Damage.

source_refs.section_title: 1.2 Safety
source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FIRE OR EXPLOSION HAZARD The furnace is designed and approved for use with Natural Gas and Propane (LP) Gas ONLY. DO NOT BURN ANY LIQUID FUEL OR SOLID FUEL IN THIS FURNACE. Burning any unapproved fuel will result in damage to the furnace's heat exchanger, which could result in Fire, Personal Injury, and/or Property Damage.

safety_warnings.text: Wear safety glasses, protective clothing, and work gloves. Have a fire extinguisher available. Read these instructions thoroughly and follow all warnings or cautions included in literature and attached to the unit. Consult local building codes as well as the current editions of the National Fuel Gas Code (NFGC) NFPA 54/ANSI Z223.1 and the National Electrical Code (NEC) NFPA 70.

source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FOLLOW ALL SAFETY CODES Wear safety glasses, protective clothing, and work gloves. Have a fire extinguisher available. Read these instructions thoroughly and follow all warnings or cautions included in literature and attached to the unit. Consult local building codes as well as the current editions of the National Fuel Gas Code (NFGC) NFPA 54/ANSI Z223.1 and the National Electrical Code (NEC) NFPA 70.

safety_warnings.text: Check entire gas assembly for leaks after lighting this appliance. Never test for gas leaks with an open flame. Use a commercially available soap solution made specifically for the detection of leaks to check all connections, as specified in the Installation, Operation, and Maintenance Manual.

source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FIRE, EXPLOSION Check entire gas assembly for leaks after lighting this appliance. Never test for gas leaks with an open flame. Use a commercially available soap solution made specifically for the detection of leaks to check all connections, as specified in the Installation, Operation, and Maintenance Manual.

safety_warnings.text: Failure to follow this warning could result in dangerous operation, serious injury, death, or property damage. Improper installation, adjustment, alteration, maintenance, or use could cause carbon monoxide poisoning, explosion, fire, electrical shock, or other conditions which may cause personal injury or property damage. Consult a qualified service agency, local gas supplier, or your distributor for information or assistance.

source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FIRE, EXPLOSION, ELECTRICAL SHOCK, AND CARBON MONOXIDE POISONING HAZARD Failure to follow this warning could result in dangerous operation, serious injury, death, lor property damage. Improper installation, ladjustment, alteration, maintenance, lor use could cause carbon monoxide poisoning, explosion, fire, electrical shock, or other conditions which may cause personal injury or property damage. Consult a qualified service agency, local gas supplier, or your distributor for information or assistance.

safety_warnings.text: See instructions for lighting/shutdown operation (as shown on a sticker directly on the inside of the furnace panel). Should the gas supply fail to shut off or if overheating occurs, shut off the gas valve to the furnace before shutting off the electrical supply.

source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FIRE, EXPLOSION See instructions for lighting/shutdown operation (as shown on a sticker directly on the inside of the furnace panel). Should the gas supply fail to shut off or if overheating occurs, shut off the gas valve to the furnace before shutting off the electrical supply.

safety_warnings.text: This product can expose you to chemicals including Lead and Lead components, which are known to the State of California to cause cancer and birth defects or other reproductive harm. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov.

source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: This product can expose you to chemicals including Lead and Lead components, which are known to the State of California to cause cancer and birth defects or other reproductive harm. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov.

safety_warnings.text: The furnaces must be kept free and clear of insulating materials. Inspect surrounding area to ensure insulation material is at a safe distance when installing furnaces or adding insulation materials. Insulation materials may be combustible. Maintain a 1 in. clearance from combustible materials to supply air ductwork for a distance of 36 in. horizontally from the furnace. See NFPA 90B or local code for further requirements. These furnaces SHALL NOT be installed directly on carpeting, tile, or any other combustible material other than wood flooring. In downflow installations, field supplied floor base MUST be used when installed on combustible materials and wood flooring. Special base is not required when this furnace is installed on industry standard Coil Assembly matching correct furnace width.

source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FIRE HAZARD The furnaces must be kept free and clear of insulating materials. Inspect surrounding area to ensure insulation material is is at a safe distance when installing furnaces or adding insulation materials. Insulation materials may be combustible. Maintain a 1 in. clearance from combustible materials to supply air ductwork for a distance of 36 in. horizontally from the furnace. See NFPA 90B or local code for further requirements. These furnaces SHALL NOT be installed directly on carpeting, tile, or any other combustible material other than wood flooring. In downflow installations, field supplied floor base MUST be used when installed on combustible materials and wood flooring. Special base is not required when this furnace is installed on industry standard Coil Assembly matching correct furnace width.

safety_warnings.text: Failure to follow this caution may result in personal injury. Sheet metal parts may have sharp edges or burrs. Use care and wear appropriate protective clothing, safety glasses and gloves when handling parts and servicing furnaces.

source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: CUT HAZARD Failure to follow this caution may result in personal injury. Sheet metal parts may have sharp edges or burrs. Use care and wear appropriate protective clothing, safety glasses and gloves when handling parts and servicing furnaces.

safety_warnings.text: Installation and service must be performed by a qualified service agency or the gas supplier.

source_refs.section_title: 2 Troubleshooting
source_refs.source_quote: WARNING: FIRE, EXPLOSION AND ASPHYXIATION HAZARD Installation and service must be performed by a qualified service agency or the gas supplier.

safety_warnings.text: Discharge body's static electricity before touching unit. An electrostatic discharge can adversely affect electrical components.

source_refs.section_title: 2.1 Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) Precautions
source_refs.source_quote: NOTICE: Discharge body's static electricity before touching unit. An electrostatic discharge can adversely affect electrical components.

extraction_notes: The troubleshooting chart for 'No Displayed Codes and No Fan' on page 7 is complex and has been interpreted into a list of possible causes and steps. Review is recommended for completeness and accuracy of this interpretation.
Air delivery CFM tables on pages 18-19 were not extracted due to their extensive nature and low priority for fault diagnosis/servicing in a concise format.
